document.source_title: PCOS has a new name: Here’s why experts say it matters for millions of women

claims.claim_text: PCOS has been renamed to PMOS to better reflect its complex nature.
claims.evidence_text: the condition, previously known as Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS), will now be called Polyendocrine Metabolic Ovarian Syndrome (PMOS

claims.claim_text: The new name reflects the condition’s broader impact across multiple systems.
claims.evidence_text: Experts say the new name reflects the condition’s broader impact across the endocrine, metabolic and reproductive systems

claims.claim_text: The prevalence of the condition in India is estimated to be between 16 and 18 percent.
claims.evidence_text: where experts estimate the prevalence of the condition to be between 16 and 18 per cent

entities.description: Led the global effort to rename PCOS.
entities.evidence_text: In a landmark global effort led by Monash University

entities.description: Prof Helena Teede is the president.
entities.evidence_text: president of the International Society of Endocrinology

entities.name: Professor Helena Teede
entities.description: Director of the Monash Centre for Health Research and Implementation.
entities.evidence_text: said Professor Helena Teede, Director of the Monash Centre for Health Research and Implementation

entities.description: President-elect of the Indian Society for Assisted Reproduction.
entities.evidence_text: Dr Madhuri Patil, president-elect of the Indian Society for Assisted Reproduction

metrics.evidence_text: affects around one in eight women globally, or more than 170 million women
